key_insight: Atomic Cluster Expansion (ACE) potentials can capture screw-dislocation physics in bcc refractory metals far better than prior interatomic and many ML potentials, with near-DFT performance for Mo and W.
engineering_relevance: These improved potentials enable more reliable prediction of flow stress, glide behavior, and kink-pair energetics for refractory-metal design under high-temperature and high-stress conditions.
method: Extended an existing DFT training database to build ACE potentials for V, Nb, Ta, Mo, and W.
Benchmarked the models against screw-dislocation properties, especially Peierls barriers and glide planes.
Evaluated kink-pair activation enthalpies using Kocks' law and a line-tension model for Nb, Mo, and W.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports substantial improvements over classical and some machine-learning potentials, with near-DFT screw-dislocation accuracy for Mo and W and improved predictions for V, Nb, and Ta.
watch_item: A single-humped Peierls barrier is not sufficient to validate dislocation transferability; database composition and element-specific energetics remain critical.
paper_title: Atomic Cluster Expansion Potentials for Screw Dislocations in BCC Refractory Metals
